# Work Packet ALB-001 — Repository Scaffolding, Conventions, CI

## 1. TASK

Create the Albion repository's implementation scaffolding: directory skeleton, the project conventions document, a zero-dependency test harness with a passing self-test, and a CI workflow that lints and tests on every push.

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME

- Directory skeleton matching the proposal's layout (see CONTEXT), with `.gitkeep` files in intentionally-empty directories.
- `CONVENTIONS.md` at the repo root (see MUST DO for required sections).
- `tests/run.sh` — a plain-bash test runner — plus `tests/lib/assert.sh` helpers and one passing self-test, `tests/test_harness.sh`.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — runs on push and pull_request: (1) shellcheck over all shell files, (2) `tests/run.sh`.
- `.gitignore` gains nothing (already handled by the conductor).

## 3. CONTEXT

- **Files to read first:** `README.md`, `docs/proposal/albion-proposal.md` §1 (Repository & Deliverable Layout — the directory spec to follow), `docs/build/orchestration.md` (how your work will be reviewed).
- **Repo state:** documentation only; you are creating the first implementation files.
- **Directory skeleton to create:** `bin/`, `env/`, `plugin/.claude-plugin/`, `plugin/skills/`, `plugin/agents/`, `plugin/hooks/`, `plugin/scripts/`, `charter/`, `manifest/`, `state/`, `telemetry/`, `bench/`, `tests/lib/`, `.github/workflows/`.
- **Environment:** development happens on macOS (bash 3.2-compatible unless a script declares `#!/usr/bin/env bash` and uses features guarded by CI's ubuntu runner); CI runs ubuntu-latest. shellcheck and bats are NOT installed locally — hence the zero-dependency harness and CI-side shellcheck.
- **Background:** Albion is an orchestration system for Claude Code (GLM-5.2 + fable-mode). This packet lays the ground that every later packet (env script, launcher, doctor, hooks) builds on and is tested by.

## 4. MUST DO

- Follow the proposal §1 layout exactly for directory names.
- `CONVENTIONS.md` must contain these sections: **Shell style** (bash, `set -euo pipefail`, shellcheck-clean, small functions, `snake_case` locals / `SCREAMING_CASE` exported env, no silent failures); **Tests** (how `tests/run.sh` discovers `tests/test_*.sh`, assert helper usage, every executable change ships with a test); **Documentation** (professional tone, no marketing language, every user-facing behavior documented); **Commits** (imperative subject ≤72 chars, body explains why); **Work packets** (one line: implementation arrives via reviewed packets — link `docs/build/orchestration.md`).
- `tests/run.sh`: discovers and runs every `tests/test_*.sh`, prints per-test PASS/FAIL and a summary line, exits non-zero on any failure. Pure bash, no external dependencies beyond coreutils.
- `tests/lib/assert.sh`: at minimum `assert_eq`, `assert_contains`, `assert_file_exists`, `assert_exit_code` — each printing a useful failure message.
- `tests/test_harness.sh`: exercises each assert helper (including one intentionally-caught failure path) so the harness proves itself.
- `ci.yml`: pin action versions; shellcheck step must cover `bin/`, `env/`, `plugin/scripts/`, `tests/` (finding files by shebang or extension); test step runs `tests/run.sh`; workflow must be syntactically valid YAML.
- All shell files you create must pass `bash -n` and be shellcheck-clean by inspection (CI will enforce).

## 5. MUST NOT DO

- Do NOT implement any launcher, env, doctor, hook, skill, or charter logic — later packets own those; directories stay empty except `.gitkeep`.
- Do NOT modify anything under `docs/` (including this brief), `README.md`, `LICENSE`, or `.gitignore`.
- Do NOT add external dependencies: no bats, no npm/package.json, no git submodules, no Makefile-required tools beyond bash/coreutils.
- Do NOT run `git commit`, `git push`, or any git state-changing command — the conductor owns git.
- Do NOT create files outside the repository working directory.

## 6. TOOLS ALLOWED

File creation/editing and shell execution within the repository workspace only.

## 7. SUCCESS CRITERIA

- [ ] `bash tests/run.sh` exits 0, reporting the harness self-test as passing.
- [ ] `bash -n` succeeds on every `.sh` file created.
- [ ] Directory tree matches the CONTEXT spec (verified by the conductor with `find`).
- [ ] `CONVENTIONS.md` contains all five required sections.
- [ ] `.github/workflows/ci.yml` present, pinned, covering lint + test.
- [ ] `git status` shows changes ONLY in the paths this brief allows.
